title: The Ajax Line
bounded_thesis: Law firms should independently preserve control over firm-specific professional interaction graphs and their derivatives because, if external AI systems are permitted to retain and generalize substantially overlapping multi-firm signals, a sector-level capability externality could erode differentiation—even though current registered evidence does not establish that Anthropic or another named provider is doing so.

included: Registered primary sources, the pinned anthropics/claude-for-legal revision, byte-exact chunks, bounded claims, objections, falsifiers, proof gaps, and unilateral governance recommendations.
excluded: Iliad and Trojan-War naming material, Move 37 as causal proof, claims about other user repositories, Karp flatten/eat rhetoric, Kirkland architecture assertions, making-a-god language, and present-tense existential-threat assertions.

export_gate.reason: Seventeen of eighteen claims remain unreviewed, quarantined, or retired; no thesis node is yet export-eligible, and human release decisions remain unresolved.

nodes.proposition: Firm-specific value can reside in context-to-choice-to-rationale-to-outcome relationships, not merely in standalone documents.

evidence_paths.limitation: Organizational-learning research is not direct evidence of technical learnability or legal ownership.
A 2000 Norwegian law-firm study does not establish current frontier-model effects.

nodes.does_not_prove: Ownership, legal protectability, uniqueness, portability, technical learnability, or economic value.

nodes.proposition: Pinned legal materials invite users to encode playbooks, fallbacks, deviations, rationales, risk posture, precedent, and house style.

evidence_paths.limitation: This exact deployment-script path establishes only a negative static-code observation.
A captured product announcement describes intended design, not a deployed tenant runtime.

nodes.does_not_prove: Actual tenant configuration, data movement, retention, training, connector execution, or deployed runtime equivalence.

nodes.proposition: Connectors, feedback, support, telemetry, or hosted features could expose professional signals if material leaves firm custody and reuse is permitted.

evidence_paths.limitation: Documented retention policy is not verified tenant behavior or a source-to-sink runtime trace.
Standard commercial terms are counterevidence but do not prove technical compliance or a negotiated agreement.

nodes.does_not_prove: That a named provider currently captures, retains, pools, trains on, or generalizes a firm's ontology.

nodes.proposition: If overlapping multi-firm professional traces are legally and technically reusable, resulting capability could reduce the scarcity of firm-specific capability.

evidence_paths.limitation: Competition-policy pathways and organizational theory do not establish actual cross-firm overlap or substitution.

nodes.does_not_prove: Present harm, provider intent, data aggregation, antitrust violation, transferability, substitutability, or inevitability.

nodes.proposition: Each firm should independently control ontology, derivatives, telemetry, evaluation, portability, and exit while preserving lawful unilateral decision-making.

evidence_paths.limitation: General FTC guidance requires fact-specific legal analysis and does not bless coordinated vendor action.
A normative rebuttal is a proposed governance position, not empirical proof of effectiveness.

nodes.does_not_prove: Guaranteed security, competitive superiority, compliance, provider wrongdoing, or business survival.

nodes.proposition: Legal-AI research should test unconventional delayed-value strategies, while treating Move 37 as analogy and benchmark motivation only.

evidence_paths.limitation: A first-party retrospective about a closed game cannot establish an analogous legal capability.

nodes.does_not_prove: That an AlphaGo moment exists in law, that Go predicts legal performance, or that private expert traces are necessary.

nodes.proposition: No evidence currently registered in this project establishes covert pooling of identifiable firm ontologies or default training on ordinary commercial content.

evidence_paths.limitation: A bounded registry absence cannot establish impossibility or describe evidence outside this project.
Contractual counterevidence does not prove zero retention, flawless compliance, or harmlessness.

nodes.does_not_prove: Impossibility, flawless compliance, zero processing or retention, absence of negotiated differences, or harmlessness.
